# services.knot

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.knot`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.knot.checkConfig` | `boolean` | Toggles the configuration test at build time. It runs in a sandbox, and therefore cannot be used in all scenarios. |
| `services.knot.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Knot authoritative-only DNS server. |
| `services.knot.enableXDP` | `boolean` | Extends the systemd unit with permissions to allow for the use of the eXpress Data Path (XDP). ::: {.note} Make sure to read up on functional [limitations](https://www.knot-dns.cz/docs/latest/singlehtml/index.html#mode-xdp-limitations) when running in XDP mode. ::: |
| `services.knot.extraArgs` | `list of string` | List of additional command line parameters for knotd |
| `services.knot.keyFiles` | `list of absolute path` | A list of files containing additional configuration to be included using the include directive. This option allows to include configuration like TSIG keys without exposing them to the nix store readable to any process. Note that using this option will also disable configuration checks at build time. |
| `services.knot.package` | `package` | The knot-dns package to use. |
| `services.knot.settings` | `YAML 1.1 value` | Extra configuration as nix values. |
| `services.knot.settingsFile` | `null or absolute path` | As alternative to `settings`, you can provide whole configuration directly in the almost-YAML format of Knot DNS. You might want to utilize `pkgs.writeText "knot.conf" "longConfigString"` for this. |
